home.title: French — Revision
home.intro: Reference for G9 IB MYP French Language Acquisition: themed vocabulary, verb tenses, key grammar, and useful phrases.

extras.title: French — Extras
extras.intro: Topics beyond the G9 syllabus — subjunctive, idioms, francophone culture.

subjunctive.title: Subjunctive mood — quick intro
subjunctive.body: Used to express doubt, emotion, necessity, or opinion. Triggered by phrases like 'il faut que', 'je veux que', 'bien que', 'avant que'. For -er verbs, take the 'ils' form, drop -ent, add: -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent. Example: 'Il faut que tu finisses' (you must finish). You'll meet it in IB DP.

idioms.title: Idioms & expressions
items: Avoir le cafard — to feel blue (lit. 'to have the cockroach')
Coûter les yeux de la tête — to cost an arm and a leg (lit. 'cost the eyes of the head')
Tomber dans les pommes — to faint (lit. 'fall into the apples')
Poser un lapin — to stand someone up (lit. 'place a rabbit')
Avoir un chat dans la gorge — to have a frog in your throat (lit. 'have a cat in your throat')
Ce n'est pas la mer à boire — it's not that hard (lit. 'it's not the sea to drink')
Mettre son grain de sel — to add one's two cents (lit. 'add one's grain of salt')

culture.title: Francophone culture
culture.body: French is spoken in 29 countries by 300+ million people: France, Belgium, Switzerland, Quebec (Canada), Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire, Tunisia, Morocco, Algeria, Vietnam (historically), and many more. La Francophonie is the international organisation of French-speaking nations. Major contributions: cinema (Truffaut, Godard), philosophy (Descartes, Sartre, Camus), food (haute cuisine), fashion (Chanel, Dior), and the Tour de France.

literature.title: Classic French literature
items: Le Petit Prince (Antoine de Saint-Exupéry, 1943) — short, profound, the most translated French book
Les Misérables (Victor Hugo, 1862) — epic novel of redemption and rebellion
Madame Bovary (Gustave Flaubert, 1856) — pioneering realist novel
Le Rouge et le Noir (Stendhal, 1830) — ambition and society

filmMusic.title: Modern film & music
filmMusic.body: Films to explore: Amélie (2001), The Intouchables (2011), La Haine (1995). Singers: Stromae (Belgian), Aya Nakamura, Indila, Christine and the Queens. Watching/listening with French subtitles is a great way to train your ear.
